person.name: Eudo de Morville
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Eudo de Morville aka 'vo de Mortvile / Moreville. He was born at Bradpole, Bridport, Dorset, England about 1203. Eudo was the son of William de Morville, who he succeeded in 1235.

His underage daughter, Matilda, was married by 30 September, 1237, therefore she was not yet 14 years old, so born after 1224, if he was in his majority when she was born, 1224-21. 

Eudo was married to Isabella. 

«b»Eudo and Isabella were the parents of:«/b»
1.) Matilda de Morville, was married to Matthew de Columbariis by 30 September, 1237;

2.) Elena de Morville, aka Helena whose marriage was given to Ralph de Gorges in 1237, was still in the king's keeping in 1244;

3.) Ivo de Moreville, father of Ellen who was married to Sir Ralph de Gorges, was also the father of a bastard daughter, Christina, who he gave in free marriage with the mill of Bradepole, and a messuage &c. outside Brideport, to Ralph de Karcia.

Eudo died before 30 September, 1237, when seisin of a moiety of his land, including the manors of Knighton, Bradpole and Wraxall, was given to his son-in-law, Matthew de Columbariis. William de Morville [Eudo's father] had held Bradpole in 1226-8, and Elena de Morville [Eudo's daughter] held it in 1244.

«b»Research Notes«/b»
In 1226-8, William de Morville held Bradpole by sergeanty of making summons and paying 11s rent by hand of the sheriff.

On 9 December, 1235, Eudo de Morville had made fine with the king by 25 marks for having seisin of the manor of Bridport, which William de Morville, his father, held of the king in chief by sergeanty, with the hundred of Redhone and the foreign hundred of Beaminster, which fell to Eudo by hereditary right, and the king had taken his homage for this. Order to the sheriff of Dorset that, having accepted security from Eudo for the aforesaid 25 marks to the king's use, he was to cause him to have full seisin of the manor of Bridport, the hundred of Redhone and the foreign hundred of Beaminster.

On 30 September, 1237, at Sherburn in Elmet, Matthew de Columbariis made fine with the king [Henry III] by 60 marks for having seisin of a moiety of the land formerly of Eudo de Morville, because Matthew took to wife Matilda, Eudo's underage daughter and coheir, and for having peace because he married Matilda without the king's licence, and an order to the sheriff of Hampshire told him to take security etc. and make over seisin to Matthew to his use and that of Matilda, his wife, and Elena, her sister, of the manor of Knighton until the manor was partitioned between them, retaining in the king's hand the dower that falls to the wife of Eudo until she has surrendered the aforesaid Elena to the king, who is of his gift. After the sheriff of Hampshire had taken security for the same 60 marks, he was to inform the sheriff of Dorset and Somerset, whom the king ordered to make over seisin of the manors of Bradpole and Wraxall, having retained the dower of Eudo's wife in the [king's] hand as stated above.

The king then granted to Ralph de Gorges the marriage of Helena, one of the daughters and heirs of Eudo de Morville and Isabella, his wife, and committed to Brother Geoffrey, almoner, and Aymer de St. Amand a moiety of the lands formerly of both Eudo and Isabella to keep to the use of Ralph and Helena for as long as it pleased the king. The sheriffs of Somerset, Dorset, Gloucestershire and Hampshire were ordered that once the lands had been partitioned, they were to cause Brother Geoffrey and Aymer to have full seisin of one moiety and Matthew de Columbariis and his wife, another of the daughters and heirs of the aforesaid Eudo and Isabella, of the other moiety, having retained in the king's hand that moiety of the land, both of the dower and of the inheritance formerly of Isabella, which fell to Matthew and his wife, until the king ordered otherwise. On 17 October, the sheriffs were ordered to take all that land into the king's hand and to keep it safely until the king ordered otherwise.

In 1244, Elena de Morville was in the keeping of the king [Henry III] who had married her to Ralph de Gorges; her land in Bradpole was worth £10 and she held of the king in chief by sergeanty of finding one man with linen armour (in lineis armis) to serve in the king's army for 40 days at her own cost, and for longer at the king's cost.

On 28 October, 40 Henry III [1255], at Winchester, a Writ to the sheriff of Dorset ordered him to take the keepers of pleas of the crown and make inquisition whether the mill of Bradepole, with a messuage &c. outside Brideport, late of Ralph de Karcia, was an escheat, and if so, how, and what was its value. An Inquisition before Adam de Wodeton, keeper of the pleas of the crown and sheriff of Dorset, found the tenements were given to Ralph de Kancia by Ivo de Mortvile in free marriage with his bastard daughter, Christina; Christina and Ralph had a son who died under age, then Christina died and Ralph held the premises according to the law of England (secundum legem Anglie). Then the inquisitors concerning alienation of serjeanties found that the mill with its appurtenances was alienated from the serjeanty of Bradepole, and extended it at a yearly value of 20s, of which the third part was arrented to the Exchequer. Meanwhile Sir Ralph de Gorges, who had married Ellen, daughter and one of the heirs of Ivo de Moreville, received the homage of his free men, among whom came Ralph de Kancia and did homage for the said mill, messuage, &c., which he held of Sir Ralph and Ellen [as of] the serjeanty of Bradepole.

zh.Bradpole, Bridport, Dorset, England: 英國英格蘭多瑟特布拉波爾
zh.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville是William de Morville I的兒子，於大約1203年出生在英國英格蘭多瑟特布拉波爾。他至少有1個女兒。他去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.Bradpole, Bridport, Dorset, England: 英国英格兰多瑟特布拉波爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville是William de Morville I的儿子，于大约1203年出生在英国英格兰多瑟特布拉波爾。他至少有1个女儿。他去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Morville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Moorfield
meanings: English: locative name from Morville (Shrops), which is recorded as Mamerfeld in the 12th century, Momerefeld in 1255, and Morfelde in 1464.

nameMeanings.name: Eudo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from uod(al) ‘riches’; probably originally a short form of the various compound names formed with this element.

en.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville was born about 1203, in Bradpole, Dorset, England, United Kingdom as the son of William de Morville I. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville est né environ 1203, à Bradpole, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William de Morville I. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville wurde um 1203 in Bradpole, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William de Morville I geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville è nato il circa 1203 nel Bradpole, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di William de Morville I. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Bradpole, Bridport, Dorset, England: イギリスイギリスドールセトブラドプオル
ja.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morvilleは約1203年にイギリスイギリスドールセトブラドプオルでWilliam de Morville Iの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼はイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville(은)는 대략 1203년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 브라드포일에서 William de Morville I의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville nasceu aproximadamente 1203, em Bradpole, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de William de Morville I. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville родился около 1203 г. в местности брадпол, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали William de Morville I. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Eudo de Morville nació aproximadamente 1203, en Bradpole, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de William de Morville I. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
